entries.summary: Rubric-stage apply/error/reconcile paths recomputed full-run progress by scanning all precreated sample_score_targets, causing 600-target families to time out and exhaust at rubric_critic=29/30.
entries.fix_summary: Made rubric-stage progress stage-local and removed inline stage advancement from the per-request apply/error hot path so early stages no longer pay later score-target fanout costs.

entries.summary: Heavy-family runs could store real rubric_critic artifacts while process_request_targets stayed exhausted and per-request run counter writes kept colliding on the shared runs row, making live status scientifically invalid even with applied outputs.
entries.fix_summary: Removed shared run/experiment counter writes from the per-request hot path, made live run summaries compute stage counts from progress snapshots, and taught process_request_targets refresh to treat stored stage artifacts as authoritative success.

entries.summary: A retry-created queued batch could be inserted after the scheduler drained, leaving the engine with one queued retry batch, no active scheduler lock, and run rows still marked running indefinitely.
entries.fix_summary: Retry-created batches now explicitly kick startScheduler, and getStuckWork now treats queued-only transport backlog with no scheduler heartbeat as a real scheduler_not_running stall.

entries.summary: Heavy 600-target runs could complete rubric_critic artifacts but never enter score_gen because reconcileRunStage advanced and faned out the next stage in one mutation, rolling back the handoff and leaving no transport.
entries.fix_summary: Run stage handoff now commits the stage advance first, then enqueueRunStage fans out downstream work asynchronously in bounded chunks, and getStuckWork surfaces stage_transition_no_transport when the handoff fails.

entries.summary: Rubric generation could exhaust a target even when the model produced a structurally usable rubric because parseRubricResponse only split stage criteria on semicolons, while real rubric outputs sometimes packed additional top-level criteria into comma-delimited clauses inside one semicolon segment.
entries.fix_summary: Rubric parsing now falls back to comma-delimited criterion recovery when semicolon splitting under-counts a stage, accepts recovered stages up to six criteria, tightens the rubric-gen prompt to require semicolon-only top-level delimiters, and classifies rubric parser failures as parse_error.
